task: I’m trying to post a used item online and want to avoid making the listing look sloppy. Can you help me tighten up the title, photos, and message I should send if someone asks for a pickup time?

prefs.explanation: He seems likely to prefer answers that get to the point quickly, without softening or over-explaining. His defiant, impatient style suggests he would dislike being talked around or treated delicately when he is asking for help.

prefs.short: Prefers short answers over long ones
prefs.explanation: He acts like someone who values speed and immediate payoff, so long responses would probably feel annoying unless they are clearly useful. He would likely want the minimum needed to act, not a lot of background or filler.
He seems best served by compact, actionable formatting rather than long prose. A clean list or step-by-step layout fits his quick-win mindset and reduces the chance he ignores useful details.

He likely prefers the assistant to collaborate with him and help him move faster, rather than taking over the whole decision. He would probably resist overly controlling guidance, but still accept support that preserves his sense of choice.

prefs.short: Accepts some pushback if it is brief and useful
prefs.explanation: He is distrustful and defiant, so heavy lecturing would likely turn him off. But his history shows he can adjust when consequences are made clear, so he may tolerate concise pushback when it is tied directly to immediate risk.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a clean, practical way to make it look solid fast.

## Title
Keep it:
- **exact item**
- **model/version**
- **key condition**
- **price if the platform allows**

**Good:**  
`Nintendo Switch OLED - Works - No Charger`

**Bad:**  
`Great deal!!! Must sell!!! Read description!!!`

## Photos
Use:
- 1 clear full-item photo
- 1 photo of the model number
- 1 photo of any damage
- 1 photo of included accessories
- 1 photo showing it powered on, if possible

Avoid:
- blurry shots
- stock photos
- cluttered background
- hiding damage

## Description
Keep it short and specific:
- exact model
- works / tested status
- damage that affects use
- missing essentials
- what’s included
- pickup only / cash / payment method if relevant

## Pickup message
Short and direct:

“Yep, I’m available at ___. I’ll send the exact pickup spot when you’re on the way. Let me know if that still works.”

If you want to be firmer:
“Available at ___. Please confirm before heading out so I’m not waiting around.”

## Simple rule
If the listing looks:
- **specific**
- **honest**
- **easy to scan**

it sells better.

If you want, I can rewrite your actual title/description into a cleaner version.

per_pref.reason: Very blunt and no-nonsense: "here’s a clean, practical way to make it look solid fast" and the pickup message is short and direct.
Mostly concise and action-focused, with compact sections and examples. It’s not ultra-minimal, but it avoids long background.
Strong use of bullets and checklists throughout: "Title," "Photos," "Description," "Pickup message," plus clear do/don’t lists.
Gives help without taking over: it offers structure and examples, then says "If you want, I can rewrite your actual title/description," preserving choice.
Includes brief pushback on sloppy listings via direct do/don’t guidance like "Avoid: blurry shots" and "hiding damage," but it stays concise and useful rather than lecturing.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — keep it plain:

## 3 title options
1. **[Item] - [Model] - [Works / Untested] - [Missing part if any]**
2. **[Brand] [Model] - [Condition] - [Pickup]**
3. **[Item] [Model] - [Tested Working] - [Damage / Missing if relevant]**

## Short description template
**Used [item], [exact model]. [Works / tested / untested]. [Any damage or missing parts]. Includes [what’s included]. Pickup [area].**

Example:
**Used PS4 Slim, CUH-2215A. Tested working. Missing controller. Includes console and power cord. Pickup only.**

## Pickup reply
**Sounds good — I’m available at ___. Message me when you’re on the way.**

If you want it a little firmer:
**Available at ___. Please confirm before you head out.**

If you want, I can also make it into a **copy-paste template for phone, console, or laptop listings**.

per_pref.reason: Very blunt and direct: "keep it plain," then gives compact templates without softening.
Short and efficient throughout; minimal explanation and only the needed templates.
Uses practical bullets and templates: "3 title options," "Short description template," "Pickup reply."
Gives help without taking over; offers options like "If you want it a little firmer" and "If you want, I can also make it..."
Includes brief pushback-style firmness: "Please confirm before you head out," but keeps it concise and useful.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are plain copy-paste versions.

## 3 title examples
- **PS4 Slim 1TB - Tested Working - Missing Controller**
- **Nintendo Switch OLED - Powers On - No Charger**
- **Dell Inspiron Laptop - Untested - Charger Included**

## Short description examples
- **Used PS4 Slim 1TB. Tested working. Missing controller. Includes console and power cord. Pickup only.**
- **Used Nintendo Switch OLED. Powers on. Small crack on screen corner. No charger included. Pickup only.**
- **Used Dell Inspiron laptop. Untested. Includes charger. One key missing. Pickup only.**

## Pickup text
- **I’m available at ___. Please message when you’re actually on the way.**
- **Available at ___. If you’re running late or changing plans, let me know before you head out.**
- **I can do ___ . Please confirm before coming so I’m not waiting around.**

If you want, I can make these sound a little more casual or a little more firm.

per_pref.reason: Very blunt and direct: "plain copy-paste versions" and short, no-frills examples get straight to the point.
Keeps it concise with compact examples instead of long explanation; the response is mostly just usable templates.
Uses practical bullets and labeled sections: "3 title examples," "Short description examples," and "Pickup text."
Provides help without taking over: "If you want, I can make these sound a little more casual or a little more firm" preserves user choice.
Includes brief pushback tied to a concrete issue, e.g. "Please message when you’re actually on the way" and "confirm before coming so I’m not waiting around," which is useful and not preachy.

- **Can do ___ . Please confirm before coming so I’m not waiting around.**

Keeps it short and minimal, with compact examples rather than long explanation.
Uses practical bullets and checklists under clear headings: "Titles," "Descriptions," "Pickup replies."
Gives help without taking over; it offers options the user can adapt, e.g. "I’m free at ___." and "Can do ___."
Includes brief pushback tied to practical issues, like "Message me when you’re actually on the way" and "Please confirm before coming so I’m not waiting around," without lecturing.
